title: morisonia singularis
side_effects: Morisonia singularis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its high concentration of alkaloids. Prolonged consumption or excessive dosage can also lead to kidney damage, liver toxicity, and even seizures in extreme cases. Additionally, it may interact with other medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, causing adverse reactions. If not handled and prepared correctly, the plant's toxic compounds can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues.
